# OpenMark

> Personal newsletter and knowledge graph from Ahmad Adi. Weekly issues on AI agents, retrieval-augmented generation, knowledge graphs, and the modern engineering stack. Sourced from a curated archive of ~13,000 bookmarks, where every claim cites a real URL.

OpenMark publishes weekly newsletter issues. Issues are composed by a multi-agent orchestrator (LangChain v1) running over a Neo4j Graph RAG store with 1024-dim pplx-embed vectors. The same content renders to web (this site) and email (via Resend) from a single markdown source.

## Content

- [Latest issue and archive](/issues/): Complete list of all newsletter issues in reverse-chronological order
- [RSS feed](/rss.xml): Subscribe via any feed reader (Feedly, Inoreader, Mailbrew, etc.)
- [Subscribe by email](/subscribe/): Weekly email digest with double opt-in

## Topics covered

- AI agent architectures: LangChain v1 `create_agent`, middleware stacks, sub-agent patterns, structured tool use, MCP
- Retrieval-augmented generation: hybrid vector + graph retrieval, embedding model choices (pplx-embed, text-embedding-3-large), reranking
- Knowledge graphs: Neo4j vector index, Cypher patterns, Louvain communities, SIMILAR_TO edges, tag co-occurrence networks
- Modern engineering stack: Azure AI Foundry deployments, model routing, prompt caching, eval pipelines
- Newsletter craft: single-source markdown to web + email, Maizzle templates, Resend deliverability

## Source code

- [OpenMark repository](https://github.com/OthmanAdi/OpenMark): The orchestrator, 10 sub-agents, and publish pipeline that produces this newsletter. Pure LangChain, no deepagents. MIT licensed.

## Optional

- [Subscribe page details](/subscribe/): Privacy and opt-in mechanics
- [Robots policy](/robots.txt): Crawl policy — open to AI crawlers because content is meant to be reachable
